project.title: 0N4R Tau and 0N4R Tau P301L Neuronal Interactomes
project.projectDescription: Human non-mutated 0N4R Tau and P301L mutant 0N4R Tau tagged at the N-terminus with eGFP were expressed in mouse (CD1) primary cortical neurons using adeno associated viruses to monitor early changes in the synaptic interactome beforethe onset of neuronal loss. Affinity purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) combined with tandem mass tagging was used to quantitatively compate the non-mutated and P301L interactomes.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Neurons were transduced at 6DIV and harvested at 13DIV. Cells were lysed in Triton-X-100 buffer and immunoprecipitation was undertaken with GFP-Trap magnetic agarsoe beads (Chromotek). Samples were reduced, alkylated and trypsin digested before labelling with tandem mass tag (TMT)ten-plex reagents (Thermofisher). Analysis by nano-LC MS/MS using triple-stage mass spectrometry (SPS-MS3) workflow on an Orbitrap Fusion Tribid mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The raw data files were processed using Proteome Discoverer Software v2.1 (PD2.1)and searched against the UniProt Mus musculus database plus GFP-Tau and GFP-Tau-P301L sequences using the SEQUEST HT Algorithm. Dats normalised to GFP and false discovery rate set at 5%. Protein abundances were Log2 transformed. Principle component analysis was calculated using the FactoMineR package.
